entry.title: Re: From idea to app: using AI-powered Project Accelerator to develop SAP Fiori apps
entry.content: Please note that starting with SAP Fiori tools 1.18.2, the staging area mentioned above has been removed from the Project Accelerator. The staging area was the place on the UI where the users could go in, view the files and then had to manually accept those files and they get copied across into the workspace and only then they are available for full use with SAP Fiori tools. Now, the project gets automatically published into the workspace after it has been generated and becomes avaiable like any other project with SAP Fiori Tools.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps in the new business model: error with undefined destination path after deployment
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I'm having trouble building and deploying an app. This is happening in a subaccount where I have a SAP Build subscription under the new business model. I have a Developer license.

In the preview, the app works perfectly, but after the deployment, it doesn't. The path that's giving me problems is this: https://prod-q7znbkxk.launchpad.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/buildapps317150.buildapps317150/undefinedB1/SalesOpportunities?%24format=json

I've checked the xs-app.json file and it shows this:
{
"source": "^/destinations/B1(.*)$",
"target": "$1",
"destination": "B1",
"csrfProtection": true
}

Another app in another subaccount has the same value in the same file and works perfectly. I checked the additional properties in my Destination and everything is exactly the same.

Has anyone had this problem before? Thanks in advance for your guidance.

Best regards.

Erick

entry.title: Re: How to short automatic packing HU in putaway
entry.content:
Hello @imel_99 

Thank you for your feedback and question. After activating 5DV (Advanced Packing) and 2EJ (Handling Unit Management) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, HU filling is controlled via the Manage Packaging Specifications app.

You should define, Packaging Instructions with packing material, quantity, weight, or volume limit, and the system prioritises full HU filling based on those capacity settings

Possible Workarounds if standard logic doesn't meet the business requirement,

  • Adjust Packaging Specifications to set strict limits, forcing full HU utilisation
  • Use different Packspec.. versions for different scenarios
  • Handle complex packing logic outside SAP using integration with 3rd party packing solutions via API

Be inform Deep packing sequence logic is restricted in S4 Public Cloud, custom development would require BTP extensions.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting - Release 2502.4 is generally available
entry.content:
We're happy to announce the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting 2502.4, delivered on June 23, 2025.

Here's what's new:

  • Conversational search with Joule – our AI copilot now lets you ask questions in natural language and get clear, summarized answers from SAP Help Portal content.
  • A refreshed Post Group Journal Entries app – now with an accountant-friendly layout and intuitive debit/credit indicators.
  • A redesigned consolidation monitor – enjoy one-click actions and advanced scheduling to save time and reduce complexity.
  • Our first release of the new consolidation rule engine – offering flexible automation for unit-dependent postings.

These improvements are built to simplify your financial closing, improve user satisfaction, and accelerate your group reporting process.

Download our feature deck.

--
Conversational search through Joule, our AI copilot

Joule is the AI copilot that truly understands your business. Joule revolutionizes how you interact with your SAP business systems, making every touch-point count and every task simpler. It enables the companion of the Intelligent Enterprise, guiding you through content discovery within the SAP Ecosystem, and giving a transparent role-based access to the relevant processes from everywhere. This is the one assistant experience, a unified and delightful user experience across SAP’s solution portfolio.

Joule offers conversational interactions between humans and computers to simplify access to information and automate business processes, thus improving both the employee and customer satisfaction. Joule can direct you to the right application to complete your tasks, help you to complete certain tasks, and find the information you're looking for quickly and easily.

Available now, Joule’s informational capability (Conversational search) in Group Reporting helps you retrieve necessary information from the SAP Help Portal or documents uploaded to the Joule service if you've Set Up Document Grounding. 

Joule searches available documentation and its own knowledge base to find the answer to your query, summarizes the search results and provides the summarization as the answer to your question, along with the top three search results. The screenshots below show conversation examples around consolidation simulations, in multiple languages.

For more information: 

  • What is Joule? | SAP Help Portal
  • Joule Capabilities | SAP Help Portal 
  • Conversational search through Joule, our AI copilot | Group Reporting 2502.4 feature deck

--
One-Click Consolidation in a New Consolidation Monitor

The consolidation monitor gives you a graphic overview of consolidation units and consolidation groups, along with tasks and task groups. You can use it as a central work environment where you're able to execute tasks for collecting, standardizing, and consolidating all reported financial data. At the same time, you can monitor the progress of these tasks for each individual consolidation unit or group.

Available now, we deliver a new version of the Consolidation Monitor. You can now work in a modern user interface powered by the latest Fiori technology, including features like filtering and sorting. To enhance your closing process experience, you're able to perform several actions with just one click – for example, you can carry out a full consolidation or open and close periods effortlessly. You can improve usability by switching from the existing app to this new version with its updated interface. You're able to increase efficiency by performing actions across multiple values, including tasks, groups, and periods. Additionally, you can streamline operations by opening or closing multiple periods, running full consolidations, and automating task processing through task scheduling.

With the new Consolidation Monitor, you're able to:

  • Filter by versions, multiple consolidation groups, periods, tasks, or statuses to quickly find what you need
  • View a list of tasks with detailed status information, such as the last execution time, who last changed it, the number of warnings or errors, the period status, and the data monitor status
  • Run a full consolidation, executing all automatic tasks
  • Process multiple consolidation groups
  • Process multiple fiscal periods by bundling them in a process period
  • Open/close periods for multiple consolidation groups and fiscal periods 
  • Define processing periods that allow you to handle multiple data-carrying periods within a single processing period periods) at once
  • Schedule a full consolidation in “Schedule Jobs for Consolidation Tasks”
  • Schedule remaining tasks, including custom tasks, to streamline your workflow
  • Schedule the entire monitor to automate and optimize further your consolidation process.
  • Perform multitask actions like running, blocking, and unblocking tasks
  • Carry out multiple period actions, including opening or closing periods

To run a full consolidation, simply select “Run Full Consolidation” (screenshot below). The parameters are automatically carried over from the filters, and all predefined consolidation tasks will be executed. If the process involves multiple consolidation groups, they will be handled in alphabetical order. 

You can also run tasks or update their status individually by selecting the task, clicking “Actions,” and choosing the appropriate option. In addition, you can select and run multiple automatic tasks together (screenshot below). To run a manual posting task, select a single manual task and click “Manual Run.”

You can still use the "old" Consolidation Monitor as a fallback. However, we recommend getting familiar with the new Consolidation Monitor in a test environment first, and then fully transitioning to it at the beginning of a new process period.

For more information: 

  • Consolidation Monitor (New Version) | SAP Help Portal 
  • New Consolidation Monitor | Group Reporting 2502.4 Feature Deck

--
New Consolidation Rule Engine - Increased Flexibility in Automating Consolidation Unit Dependent Postings

As part of our roadmap acceleration program, we’re developing a new consolidation rule engine in Group Reporting. This engine is inspired by SAP Financial Consolidation and aims to offer the same level of power and flexibility valued by its users. The delivery will occur in three phases. With this release (2502.4) we’re introducing the automation of consolidation unit dependent postings. Moving forward in 2025, our plans include the automation of inter-company postings and the automation of consolidation group-dependent postings.

With release 2502.4, you can now define flexible rules to automate consolidation unit dependent postings on non-consolidated data (posting levels 00, 0C, 01, 10). These rules can replace the existing tasks of annual net income and reclassifications. For example, you can calculate the net income based on the reported data and specify which additional or custom fields should be retained. You can also enrich the reported data by factoring in the G/L account and assigning a specific partner unit. Additionally, for selected consolidation units, you can reclassify Inventory of Finished Goods as Inventory of Semi-Finished Goods.

Our new rule definition framework provides you with improved usability, increased flexibility and robustness. You’re now able to:

  • Define source data flexibly
  • Assign multiple document types in a single task
  • Create multiple posting lines
  • Define target data flexibly, including inheritance from selected data and value calculation
  • Assign document types at the individual rule level
  • Include results from previous rule steps as an option
  • Assign rules to rule groups
  • Assign rule groups to tasks
  • Utilize a modern task log

The key apps you can use to manage automatic postings are:

(1) Manage Rules for Automatic Postings - Use this app to create, change, copy and display posting rules and rule groups and activate them for the automatic posting of group journal entries.

(2) Assign Rule Groups for Automatic Postings - Use this app to assign rule groups to consolidation tasks. The consolidation task then carries out the rules included in the assigned rule group. 

(3) Import/Export Current Settings - Use this app to export your specified  posting rules or posting rule groups to a spreadsheet, or import any rules or rule groups exported from another system.

(4) Task Logs - Use this app to check the logs of each consolidation task along the consolidation process.

When you open the "Manage Rules for Automatic Postings" app, you can search and filter rule groups and rules using various criteria, including a search bar, editing status (such as All, Drafts, or Locked), status (Active or Inactive), chart of accounts, and the user who created or last changed the rule.

Managing rules

To create a rule, just click on "Create". As shown in the screenshot below, in a rule, you define the source and target data, and the posting lines.

(1) Source Data – You can define how to select the source data using a selection object, a period selection, and the fields to read. You're able to restrict the source data by selecting a range of master data, defining a period interval, and choosing specific fields to read, which improves rule performance and log readability.

(2) Target Data – You can choose to post on the original document type or a single document type that you select. You're able to select which measures must be posted by the rule, overriding the document type selection. This ensures accurate posting of values in the target data.

(3) Posting Lines – The overview summarizes the posting line definition, and clicking on a posting line displays its details. You can define the field values and calculate the amounts to post. You're able to edit details like field assignments and value calculations for each posting line. 

Managing rule groups

You can define Rule Groups to select and organize the rules that need to be executed when running an automatic postings task in the Data Monitor. For example, you're able to use the Rule Group NI_RCL to execute three rules: calculating net income (for both released and reported data), and reclassifying inventory of finished goods. You can assign the Rule Group to the task using the "Assign Rule Groups for Automatic Postings" app.

From the overview page, open your rule group (1). The rule group page displays nodes (2) and rules (3). When clicking a rule, you can navigate to its definition. You can define that one rule depends on previous rules. This is needed when the source data of the current rule includes data posted by the previous rule.

Import / export settings

Additionally, you can export the rule groups, rules, rule group assignments, and selections from one system to import them in a different system. This is particularly useful when you want to export the validation or posting settings that are defined, activated, and tested in the system you use for testing and import them to your production system.

For more information:

  • Automation of consolidation unit dependent postings | SAP Help Portal
  • Automation of consolidation unit dependent postings | Group Reporting 2502 feature deck
  • Group Reporting Roadmap Acceleration Program | SAP Blog
  • Consolidation auto-post rules engine (new rule engine)  | SAP Roadmap Explorer 

--
Extending Usability in Creating Manual Journals Using a Debit/Credit Layout

With release 2502.4, we enhance the usability of the "Post Group Journal Entries" app. You can choose to enter manual group journals with a Debit/Credit indicator and sign-free amounts. When you activate this new layout, the manual journal entry is more accountant-oriented. This new feature changes only the display of the journals; it doesn’t change the way they're stored in the database. It's also applied to previously posted journals.

(1) When you activate the Debit/Credit indicator in the Post Group Journal Entries app, you can now see a new column called “Debit/Credit”, which lets you choose between two values: “Debit” or “Credit.” 

(2) You're able to enter the amounts in local, group, or transaction currency, as well as the quantity, without needing to use a sign. 

(3) A new “Balance” section lets you view the balance by field type, currency/unit, and version (for extension versions). For local and group currency, you’ll see a green highlight when the entry is balanced (total is zero), and a red highlight when it’s not. The amounts in transaction currency and quantity don’t need to be balanced, so they aren’t color-coded.

For more information: 

  • Debit/Credit Indicator in the Manual Group Journal Entries Apps | SAP Help Portal 
  • Debit/Credit Indicator | Group Reporting 2502 feature deck

--
More information

  • Group Reporting 2502.4 Feature Deck | sap.com
  • Group Reporting | SAP Help Portal
  • Group Reporting new features recap | SAP Community
  • Group Reporting community | SAP Community
  • Group Reporting customer influence platform | SAP Customer Influence

entry.title: Re: Variable pay based on different base dates in prorate...
entry.content:
I am not going to be able to provide code for the pay component solution but I will detail how I would go about it.  * Note component names and ID's are not actual, just examples.

1. Say you use a pay component such as Annualized Salary (1000) for you salary.   You would create a new pay component, Bonus Salary (1010)

2. When you have a change in Annualized Salary (1000), you initiate a rule that would update Bonus Salary (1010).  That rule will look at the effective date (specifically the month and day) and based on the effective date, you will determine if the Bonus Salary (1010) pay component needs to be changed.

3. When you map fields to the employee history for the bonus calculation, you will map to Bonus Salary (1010) and not  Annualized Salary (1000).

entry.title: Re: Issue in developing GSTR2 report
entry.content:
Hello @sapcomyogik 

Thanks for the follow-up. I completely understand the importance.GSTR2 reporting can be tricky in S/4HANA Public Cloud due to limited flexibility with CDS views.

Recommend approve

  • Use I_OperationalAcctDocTaxItem to extract tax amounts and tax codes
  • Join it with I_JournalEntry for header information
  • Avoid using I_JournalEntryItem for tax reporting, as amounts may differ due to how tax lines are distributed

If the tax data still doesn’t align, the next step is to raise an SAP Incident for support to review your setup.

With kind regards

entry.title: Re: Project Plan Cost are different
entry.content:
Hi Keratiya,

Please check your Financial Statement Version (FSV) to ensure that all the accounts you used are properly mapped. It’s likely that some accounts are not mapped in the FSV.

The project cost report runs based on the Financial Statement Version (FSV), whereas the P&L report does not.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Data Extraction from SAP Business ByDesign for migration to Cloud ERP Public
entry.content:
In this blog post, lets delve into the extraction of data from SAP Business ByDesign for the purpose of migrating the data to Cloud ERP Public. I have collated our experience and learnings on data extraction after working in more than 5 transition projects, from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP Cloud ERP Public.

Overview:

Customers who are outgrowing their SAP Business ByDesign are most commonly opting for the modern and robust ERP system from SAP i.e., Cloud ERP Public, previously referred to as SAP S/4HANA Cloud. To move from one ERP to other customers need to extract their data from source ERP system and move the same to target ERP.

SAP Business ByDesign has an inbuilt data extraction, self-service tool, that supports extraction of the data from the most common objects like master data, transaction data and accounting data. The tool is available in the work center Application and User Management.

Users need access to the work center view Data Extraction to extract data using this tool.

 

 

For more details on the data extraction search for data extraction in the SAP Business ByDesign help document library. Makes sure to select the latest version before delving into the content.

Best Practices:

There are numerous suggestions on how to extract the data effeciently and overcome challnges. I would highlight some of the key best practices that you should keep in mind to have quick and successful extarction to avoid efforts in data cleansing and transformation.

  • As far as possible use the data extraction tool, if the tool supports the extraction of data for the object which you are intrested in. The tool will provide the data in XML form which is very similar to the form that the target system needs for upload.The tool supports extarction for the most common objects. There are nearly 80 extraction obejcts supports in this tool
  • Always extract the data you need. Do not try to take dump of whole data toa void issues with perforamnce and memory. You should try to use the filters provided for extraction, per object, in the best possible way. For example, normally there is no need to extract the price lists that are not valid as of today or not yet released. You can always keep you SAP Business ByDesign system for reference to historical data for a certain number of years after migration by opting for license of SAP Business ByDesign in Archive mode.
  • If you have to extract the whole set of data and the data volume is too huge, always set the PackageActive flag and define a suitable Package Size (Instance) parameter. This will extract all instances of, for example Materials, in multiple files. Each file will get the number of instances defined in package size. This will avoid memory overflow and timeout issues.
  • In case you are still facing memory and/or time-out issues in extraction after setting the package size, please raise an incident to suitable SAP Business ByDesign component for data extraction and provide the details of the extarction with screen shot. After doing the analysis, the relevant team shall take a call if there is a need to extend the memory of the system to allow you data extraction. If needed, memory will be normally extended for 15 days period.
  • It is advisable to run heavy extraction during non-business hours to avoid loading the system with heavy tasks thus avoiding system performance.
  • If the extraction is not supported for an object or the extraction does not provide result in the way you want for migration to SAP Cloud ERP Public, then you can opt for reporting capabilities of SAP Business ByDesign. It is recommended to use reports to extract the financial data like open Payable, open Receivables, GL Balances, Fixed Asset Postings etc. For details about the reports and how to use them refer to our guide provided with the integration flows alongside the object in Busienss Accelerator hub.

Improvements in Extraction:

After working on several transition projects, we have channelized the feedback to the relevant teams for improvements in data extraction. There have been good improvements in data extraction and more shall be planned in future. Some of the key improvements that are made or in progress are:

  • It shall be possible to download all the extracted files for an extraction object run in one single click. Earlier it was only possible to download one file at a time. This has enhanced the user experience and saved lot of clicks and time.
  • Delete action on downloaded file did not provide any warning. This led to irreversible action and caused to rerun extraction if a file is accidently deleted. Users shall be now warned on deletion.
  • Various objects are enhanced with more meaningful filters to avoid filtering after extraction. Example Materials can be extracted based on Plant and Sales Organization. Price Lists can be extracted based on status and validity.
  • Extraction of attachments is not possible currently as a self-service. It was only supported y operations which was a time-consuming activity. Soon in the future, we will enable the extraction of attachments as a self-service.

What next:

Should you have any suggestions for improvements or new feature requirements in data extraction from SAP Business ByDesign please post your topics as comments to thus blog and we will be happy to take it forward for evaluation.

Thanks for your interest in the data extraction and hope this helps in your transition projects from SAP Business ByDesign to Cloud ERP Public.

entry.title: Re: Issue with next inspection stage in Quality level
SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) Q3 Update
entry.content:
The SAP Enterprise Support portfolio of services and offerings delivers foundational support and is included with every SAP cloud subscription—at no additional cost.

ESAC is our innovation program focused on continuously improving SAP Enterprise Support content across all cloud solutions by involving you directly in collaboration and feedback.

By joining the program, you will:
· Gain access to an exclusive circle of industry peers and receive hands-on expert advice
· Become an early adopter of our latest support innovations and ideas

This is a great opportunity to innovate, pilot, and help shape SAP Enterprise Support content with a select group of peers—while gaining valuable support for your own ongoing projects.

🔹No New Workstreams in Q3

There are no newly introduced workstreams planned for Q3.

🔹Workstreams Still Open in Q3

The following workstreams remain open to customers this quarter:

  • Continuous Quality Check*: Deployment Readiness for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
  • Continuous Quality Check: GoingLive Support for SAP Cloud Integration
  • Continuous Quality Check: Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP Tactical Supply Planning (Response and Supply or Sales and Operations)
  • Expert Guided Implementation: SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Generative AI (only for customers in EMEA and APJ)

👉View the workstream overviews

The following services will become generally available in Q3 (from Q2 workstreams):

  • Expert-guided Implementation: Selective Data Transfer (SDT) to SAP Cloud ALM
  • Expert-guided Implementation: SAP Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code
  • Expert Guided Implementation: Activate Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition
  • Continuous Quality Check: Data Volume Management (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

For more information, please refer to ESAC on the SAP Support Portal.
To express interest or join the program: contact us via esac@sap.com.

Your SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council team

*Continuous quality check (CQC) = work with our experts to analyze your system and/or situation based on real life system or solution data. You will receive a service report with an executive summary, findings and a detailed action plan to improve or mitigate risks.

 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

Alexander Daum is the global head of the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) program. He has held various positions in development, consulting, product management and services & support in SAP since he joined the company in 1998.  As a Design Thinking coach and certified Usability Engineer, he's a strong advocate of the user centric approach, which is also reflected in the way EASC is run today.

entry.title: Re: Thresholds Prevent Unintended Deletion of Users when Provisioning with SAP Cloud Identity Servic

Thank you.

One option would be to use the deleteEntity scope, which will only deactivate the user in the target SAP Enable Now. For more information, refer to the scope -> deleteEntity in the Transformation Expressions. However, if you run a new provisioning job from MS Entra ID (formerly, Azure), I believe it will recreate the user in IAS and sync them again with SAP Enable Now.

Best regards,

Ivelina

Jordi

entry.title: Unable to create "Packing" on credit blocked delivery
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When we create delivery which is blocked due to credit check, system does not allow us to  select the handling unit  in the "pack" process of the delivery. Standard behavior is not to allow picking for such credit blocked delivery and this is understandable. I am not even changing the pick quantity. By default at time of delivery creation the pick quantity is blank. 

Warehouse users wants to be able to create a packing step for such credit blocked delivery before picking it and actually shipping the product to customer.

I have given the screen shots on the enclosed document with the behavior of "pack" before and after credit release .

We have migrated from ECC 6.0 to  S4 HANA 2023 On Premise . 

Trust experts would be able to guide me to a quick resolution.

Thanks,

Kartik Shah

Before and After credit check status for the "Pack" the delivery items.

Status of delivery- Credit Block – Credit check executed document not Okay.

Packing status – Blank ( Not Relevant)

When trying to perform “pack” I get this screen with no option to select any handling unit

Once the credit block is released using VKM4 for the delivery we now have the status as Approved by Credit Representative

Once the credit block is removed and I perform the “pack” process I am able to select the handling unit on the packing screen but still shows the same message HUDIALOG017

entry.title: Issue with provisioning user assignment to Subaccount Administrator role in BTP Subaccount
entry.content:
Hello All,

Based on best practice advice from SAP I am using the custom identity provider set by the property "xsuaa.origin: sap.custom" when provisioning users and role collection assignment from SAP CIS Identity Directory as source system to a SAP BTP Subaccount (SAP BTP XS Advanced UAA (Cloud Foundry)) target system. this work fine for all role collections except for the Subaccount Adminstrator role. 

SAP has build in a behaviour for this role that you cannot delete the last user assigned with the default identity provider. 

3281088 - Delete user from BTP Cloud Foundry subaccount fails with error "Failed to delete the user (400)" - SAP for Me

  • error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://api.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/Groups/Subaccount%20Administrator with statusCode: 400, Response: {"error_description":"Cannot remove users from role collection Subaccount Administrator in identity zone 9e4027ca-2201-4025-af99-5346f82b47ab. At least one user must be present in the role collection.","error":"scim","message":"Cannot remove users from role collection Subaccount Administrator in identity zone 9e4027ca-2201-4025-af99-5346f82b47ab. At least one user must be present in the role collection."},

I assume that the scim provisioning wants to remove the user with the default identity provider assignment and provision the users assigned to the Subaccount Administrator role in the identity directory and set the custom identity provider. 

Do you have the same experience with this role? How do you prevent the removal of the last user with the default identity provider. Do you handle this in the transformation mapping? The current almost default SAP transformation results in an error in the log, which I would like to resolve...

Any pointers are much appreciated!

Regards,

Guido

entry.title: Sap Service Cloud v1. forward ticket to another e-mail channel
entry.content:
Hallo community,

we are struggling with a problem regarding the ticket forward to other email channels in the service cloud. 

Let's say team A work with e-mail channel teamA@xxx.com and team B with the email channel teamB@xxx.com . Each team has access to his own tickets. Team A gets an e-mail and ticket 01 is created. Team B must do something to help Team A with this issue. The ticket should remain to team A because there are some other people involved and they do not want to lose sight of the ticket. They try to forward the email to channel teamB@xxx.com. The forwarded email is sent with the ticket id (this can not be deleted from the subject) so when the email comes back to the system, team B doesn't get the message. This is just visible under the original ticket (which team B cannot access) - a new ticket is not created for team B.

I thought about Team  A creating a new ticket for team B as following activity. I'm pretty sure, my colleagues wont't like the idea of doing some extra work .. I mean in Outlook, you just forward the email and there is nothing else to do.

Anyone having the same problem? How did you lose it?

Any help will be appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

Giorgia

entry.title: Re: Tips and Tricks – Time Valuations with next day combinations (working or non working day filters
entry.content:
Hi @NeeleshKamath 

Thank you for your comments and response.

Currently there is  a requirement to evaluate the start time of the next day’s planned working hours and compare it with the recorded times (e.g., overtime or callouts). If there is any overlap between these recorded hours and the next day’s planned working time, the overlapping duration should be deducted from the planned time, as the employee will already be compensated for that period at an overtime or callout premium rate.

Example:

  • Planned working time: 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM

  • Overtime/Callout: 11:00 PM to 8:00 AM (next day)

  • In this case, the 2-hour overlap from 6:00 AM to 8:00 AM should be deducted from the planned time.

At present, we are able to retrieve the end time of the recorded hours but are unable to compare it against the next day’s planned working time.

entry.title: AUoM-Based Warehouse Task Splitting in SAP EWM?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on a requirement in SAP EWM where we need to split warehouse tasks (WTs) based on the alternate unit of measure (AUoM) defined in the product master. The goal is to optimize picking by creating WTs in the largest possible UoM first — for example, Pallet > Case > Each — and then cascade down based on available stock and delivery quantity.

Here’s a simple example of the AUoM setup in product master:

  • 1 Case = 10 EA
  • 1 Pallet = 100 EA

If the delivery quantity is 312 EA, the system should ideally create:

  • 1 WT for 3 Pallets (300 EA)
  • 1 WT for 1 Case (10 EA)
  • 1 WT for 2 Each (2 EA)

Let me know your thoughts on effectively achieving this

entry.title: SAP TM public cloud manually change between FTL and LTL
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In our S/4HANA Public Cloud tenant we use Transportation Management.
When I create a Road Freight Order I only get one document type – SFO2 (Road Freight Order) – and it is hard-wired to Shipping Type 18 (FTL).
The Shipping Type field itself is read-only, and the usual SSCUI “Define Freight Order Types” (103983) does not exist in Public Cloud.

Questions

  1. What is the supported way to make Shipping Type 17 (LTL) available?

  2. Do we have to raise a Request Expert Configuration so SAP copies SFO2 and sets Shipping Type 17, or is there any self-service alternative?

  3. Could condition-based FO-type determination switch between FTL and LTL at planning/tender time?

  4. Any best-practice tips from projects that already solved this?

(See screenshot below – Shipping Type is locked on FTL.)

 

The ultimate situation I would like is if I can manually choose between FTL or LTL. 

Thanks for any insights!

Best regards,
Floris

entry.title: Re: The PR/PO does not commit the budget
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

First of all, just to clarify — we’re talking about S/4HANA Public Cloud, not the Private Edition. The Public version handles budgeting differently and has already evolved. 😄

That said, I believe this might be a program error. I recommend opening an incident with SAP, as this is a relatively new feature and may still have some issues. I have access to a current version and have already noticed some problems with commitments.

Given that, I don't think the issue is related to the Availability Control (AVC) itself, but rather to how commitments are tagged semantically. You can test this by opening the “Project Costs Report” app — check if the commitment appears there. Most likely, it won’t. The same behavior probably happens in the “Project Budget Report” app.

In my opinion, this is the root cause: there’s a logic error in the Public Cloud version that fails to properly determine commitments.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: APM Role uses
entry.content:
What permissions do each of the following roles give a user?

AdminFlexKeyUser

FlexKeyUser

FlexPublicViewEditor

KeyUser

MeasurementDocument_Create

MeasurementDocument_Read

MeasurementDocument_Sync

RuleRepositorySuperUser

RuleRuntimeSuperUser  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: APM Role uses
entry.content:
Roles AdminFlexKeyUser, FlexKeyUser, FlexPublicViewEditor, KeyUser are for creating key user extensions.

This link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APM/f3dde866a96d4638bd2ac2f18ea83cbc/025f7a4eb0f74126b09c0651ac9c4b9e.html doesn't go into an what each role does specifically, but what the roles in general allow a user to do.

 

Roles MeasurementDocument_Create, MeasurementDocument_Read & MeasurementDocument_Sync are for certain rules API's. These API's are not public API's but the system does use them. You won't find them on the accelerator hub.

 

Role RuleRepositorySuperUser gives you:

  • Access to the Manage Rule Projects application and repository APIs to model and manage rules.

  • The permission to grant access to a business rules project for other users using privilege APIs. Go to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and choose Privileges on the left menu.

Role RuleRuntimeSuperUser gives you access to deploy and execute the ruleservices. 

For more rule roles please see: https://help.sap.com/docs/business-rules/business-rules-capability-for-cloud-foundry-environment/authorization-configuration

entry.title: Archiving not possible for application V6: Object type is missing
entry.content:
We have a requirement to archive Handling Unit into ArchiveLink server. We have successfully configured the same in systems and shipment document when saved, output is triggered and archiving is being done. But for associated HU, we are unable to issue output with medium- External Send and Storage mode- Print or Print & Archive. Any solution on this please?

System throws error when issuing output against HU from VT02N or from VL74 that 

Archiving not possible for application V6: Object type is missing

Message no. VN049

Diagnosis

Optical archiving requires an object type. You can define the object type per output application in Table 681Z.

Procedure

You must include a valid object type for the output application in Table 681Z.

 

It seems no entry is available in T681Z table against V6 application along with Hdtable- KOMKBV6. Where object type- BUS3070 must be available. Like for V7 we have VTTK object type is available in this same table and no issue is there. 

 

What is the configuration path or solution to make an entry against BUS3070 object for Handling unit over here?

entry.title: Key User Custom Fields: How to add a custom field to a specific Fiori UI section
entry.content:
Have you ever created a key user custom field and wanted to display it in a standard SAP Fiori UI, but didn't know which OData service entity to enable to make the field available in the field list offered by Fiori Runtime Adaptation?

If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. It explains how you can find out the respective OData service entity manually (called Variant 1: Manual way). It also explains a much simpler alternative how to enable the OData service entity for the given field using the integration between Fiori Runtime Adaptation and the key user custom field tool (called Variant 2: Automatic way).

Variant 1: Manual way

The first step is to identify which OData service entity provides the data for the UI section in the Fiori UI of the SAP standard application. Once you have this information, you can enable the custom field in the Custom Fields app for the specified OData service entity. This action makes the custom field available in the list of fields within Fiori Runtime Adaptation.

Let’s assume that you created a custom field named “Priority” on the business context SD_SALESDOC, and you want to expose this custom field in the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 (Fiori app ID F3893). You want to place the field in the “Basic Data” section of the “General Information” section of the Sales Order UI (the location is marked with a red cross within the screenshot).

You want to place the custom field here

First, you need to find out which OData service entity provides the data for the UI section where you want to place the field.
You can find this out by adhering to the following steps:

  1. Open the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 and navigate to the respective screen within this UI (in our case, it’s the detail screen of the sales order)
  2. Use the key combination CTRL + SHIFT + ALT + S. A Fiori Diagnostics dialog pops up
  3. Within the dialog UI, open the Control Tree section
  4. Go back to the “General Information Basic Data” section of the Sales Order UI and press CTRL + ALT + SHIFT. Then, pick the UI section or field you are interested in within the application UI. In our example, we used the “Order Reason” field (make sure that you select the field and not its label). The control in the control tree representing this field is selected
  5. Now, you can select the “Binding Info” tab shown on the right side of the control tree screen. In the “path” field, you’ll see the name “SalesOrderManage” of the OData entity where the field is bound against (see subsequent screenshot).

Find the OData entity set name within the binding information for a field

Equipped with this information, you can now navigate back to the custom fields tool. Go to the details screen of your custom field (in our example, it’s the field “YY1_Priority”) and open the “User Interfaces” tab. Enter “SalesOrderManage” in the search bar.
This leads to the following result:

Find the OData entity set name within the binding information for a field

In the “Data Source” column, you’ll now see the “SalesOrderManageType” data source. This corresponds to the entity set name “SalesOrderManage”. The different suffix “Type” is used because in the Custom Fields app, the entity type is shown instead of the entity set name.

There may be cases where several OData services share the same OData entity set name, and all of them support exposing the respective custom fields. In this case, you can find out which of the search results is the right one by comparing the description of the OData service entry in the custom fields tool with the info you see in the “Inherited from” field within the Binding Infos tab of the control tree. Since only the technical name and not the description is shown, it won’t match exactly, but in a lot of cases it’s quite similar.

Alternatively, you can compare the names in the Custom Fields app with the ones contained in the Configuration section within Implementation Information tab of the relevant Fiori app in SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library:

OData service(s) related to Fiori app

Clicking “Enable Usage” and publishing the field makes it available for the respective UI section using Runtime Adaptation.

Variant 2: Automatic way

Please note that you can also use the integration between UI Runtime Adaptation and the custom fields tool to make this process easier:

  1. Open the Fiori app Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 and navigate to the respective screen within this UI (in our case, it’s the detail screen of the sales order)
  2. Switch into Runtime Adaptation mode by selecting the “Adapt UI” option in the drop-down menu. You can get to the drop-down menu by clicking on your avatar on the top right icon representing your user
  3. Select the field or section you’re interested in. Right-click to select “+ Add: Field” from the drop-down menu
  4. A popup displays all available fields and business contexts. Mostly, there is only one – in our case it’s “Sales: Sales Document”. A “Create” option on the right top of the popup allows you to select the “Custom Field” sub-option. Clicking “Create” you’ll be navigated to the Custom Fields app. The Custom Fields app now knows from which UI section you’ve navigated to it. It also understands which OData service entity was bound against the UI section from where you came from. The business context is also taken as pre-selection information.
  5. If you create a new custom field, then the respective OData service entity is enabled automatically. If you navigate to the details of an already existing field, you’ll get a popup asking whether you want to enable the respective OData service entity you are coming from. When you select “Yes”, this will be done automatically for you (see screenshot below):

Popup allowing you to “automatically” enable the respective OData service entity for the given custom field

In case you select “No”, when you navigate back to the “User Interfaces” tab, the mentioned OData service and the OData entity will be marked with a blue bar to make it easier for you to find (see screenshot below).

OData service and OData entity marked in the Custom Fields app

Now, you’re done, and you’ve found out which OData service entity to enable to make the field available in the field list offered by Fiori Runtime Adaptation.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide them in the comments section below. Also, please follow my profile to get updates when I publish more posts on these topics.
Also feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

I want to thank Yasmina van Rooij  and Karsten Schaser for their extremely helpful contributions.

Other related blog posts and other infos:

  • https://blogs.sap.com/2022/06/23/easily-manage-your-key-user-adaptations-with-sapui5-flexibility/
  • SAP Help - Adapt UI
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-screen-personas-how-to-position-key-user-custom-fields-exactly-where/ba-p/13553723

entry.title: Cannot create variant with UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service and Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I want to enable the variant management for a Fiori List Report running standalone using the UI5 Flexibility for Key Users service.
I followed the documentation for using the service with standalone applications (https://help.sap.com/docs/ui5-flexibility-for-key-users/ui5-flexibility-for-key-users/developing-applications-running-standalone?locale=en-US)
I added the flexibility service to the bootstrap and the route to the xs-app.json.

The /settings and /data requests work fine, but when I try to save a variant the service returns HTTP 500

This is the request body for the POST request /keyuser/flex/all/v3/changes?sap-language=en

[{"reference":"<appname>","namespace":"apps/<appname>/variants/","projectId":"<appname>","support":{"generator":"FlexObjectFactory.createFlVariant","user":"<username>","sapui5Version":"1.124.11"},"originalLanguage":"EN","layer":"USER","fileType":"ctrl_variant","fileName":"id_1751547421622_174_flVariant","content":{},"texts":{"variantName":{"value":"test","type":"XFLD"}},"favorite":true,"executeOnSelection":false,"contexts":{},"variantReference":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::PageVariantManagement","variantManagementReference":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::PageVariantManagement"},{"changeType":"addCondition","reference":"<appname>","namespace":"apps/<appname>/changes/","projectId":"<appname>","packageName":"$TMP","support":{"generator":"FlexObjectFactory.createFromFileContent","user":"<username>","sourceChangeFileName":"id_1751547411208_167_addCondition","sapui5Version":"1.124.11"},"originalLanguage":"EN","layer":"USER","fileType":"change","fileName":"id_1751547421623_175_addCondition","content":{"name":"createdAt","condition":{"operator":"TODAY","values":[],"validated":"NotValidated"}},"texts":{},"selector":{"id":"<appname>::<entity>--fe::FilterBar::<entity>","idIsLocal":false},"dependentSelector":{},"variantReference":"id_1751547421622_174_flVariant"}]

There is no additional information in the response, I also did not find any further information in the logs of the approuter or the audit logs.

I tried with the SAPUI5 version 1.124.9 and 1.136.1, both are not working. My user has assigned all roles provided by the service.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to delete fs item
entry.content:
Hi @ArnoldWarhonowicz ,

May be try couple of things

A. Ensure the FS item is not in use with posted data
Even after removing it from the global settings, you cannot delete the FS item if:

It has posted data (actuals or plan)
It is used in consolidation group transactions
It is referenced in elimination entries or journal entries

Use the Group Data Analysis or Group Journal Entries apps to verify if the FS item was used in past postings.


B. Delete or deactivate
Once it's no longer used in settings or transactions:

Go to the Define FS Items app
Locate the FS item
Delete or set the status to inactive (if deletion is not permitted)

Last try:-

SSCUI 102639 – Define Consolidation of Investments Settings
Accessible via Manage Your Solution > Configure Your Solution
Look for investment-related mappings that include your FS item.Make a change if required.

I cannot think of any other possibiity apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.


Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Monitoring Non-SAP Application Via Cloud ALM
entry.content:
One of SAP’s core strategic goals is to empower IT teams by simplifying complexity and seamlessly integrating diverse systems, whether they’re SAP or not. In today's enterprise environments, organizations rely on a wide array of tools, platforms, and custom applications. Recognizing this, SAP aims to provide a centralized, unified platform where all of these components, cloud-native services, third-party systems can be monitored, managed, and optimized from one place: Cloud ALM.

This is a powerful cloud-native solution built to manage the entire lifecycle of both SAP and non-SAP applications, from initial deployment to ongoing operations. With its real-time insights and proactive capabilities, it gives you a holistic view of your IT landscape, helping ensure system health, detect issues early, and maintain governance regardless of where your workloads are running or how your architecture is structured.

 

 

What does this blog covers?

In this post, we’ll dive into how SAP Cloud ALM can integrate with non‑SAP applications, and review the available scenarios and methods to connect external systems and fully leverage its monitoring capabilities.

While SAP Cloud ALM already offers out-of-the-box (plug-and-play) integrations with well-known platforms like Microsoft Teams, Jira, ServiceNow, Tricentis, Signavio, and WalkMe, it also provides flexible integration paths for any non‑SAP solution. Let’s explore some key facts:

Cloud ALM Integration Options for Non‑SAP Applications:

1. Open Platform Architecture
SAP Cloud ALM is built on an open architecture and can monitor any non‑SAP application capable of sending telemetry data, such as metrics, logs, and traces, using OpenTelemetry Protocol.

2. Support for Custom Apps on SAP BTP
It can seamlessly monitor custom applications developed in SAP BTP, whether on Cloud Foundry or Kyma runtime, using Java or Node.js.

3. Support multiple monitoring modules in Cloud ALM
Integration & Exception Monitoring is the primary module for ingesting data (logs, metrics, traces) from non‑SAP sources.
Health Monitoring can also be used for custom applications running in SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, particularly for Java and Node.js services.

4. Native Support for OpenTelemetry
Any application, SAP or not, that supports OpenTelemetry (OTLP protocol) can send telemetry to Cloud ALM using the Raw Data Inbound API. This also allows integration with modern, cloud-native platforms which are not hosted in SAP BTP environments.

In summary:

Due to the fact these are all external (non‑SAP) systems, there are no pre-built native connectors. Instead, integration is performed via robust and secure APIs offered by SAP Cloud ALM:

  • Inbound API: for sending structured events and exception messages
  • Raw Data Inbound API (OpenTelemetry): for sending telemetry data such as metrics, logs, and traces
  • Analytics, Landscape & Event APIs: for reading system data and enabling external dashboards or alerts

API integration is the standard approach. There are no native connectors for non-SAP platforms.

How to get started

Here are the official guides to help you implement any scenario described above:

Adding Non-SAP Services and Systems | SAP Help Portal

OpenTelemetry@SAP

SAP Cloud ALM APIs for Operations

Integration & Exception Monitoring - Setup & Configuration

SAP ALM API & Integration: Open Telemetry Support ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello all, 

We are facing a challenge with "Schedule Contract Settlement - Customer Contracts" app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. 

We are looking to schedule the Delta Accruals settlement to run recurringly (daily), considering only the period up to the execution date. 

However, we've encountered the following behavior: 

      - The app requires a fixed settlement date.

      - If a broad date range is entered (e.g 01.01 - 31.12), the system processes the entire period at once.

      - There does not appear to be an option to use a dynamic date

We would like to know if there is any official or recommended approach to schedule Delta Accruals with a dynamic settlement date (e.g "today"). 

Thank you in advance for any guidance.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Business Objects with Microsoft Fabric
entry.content: Ah, that scenerio typically is due to a prompt has been triggered but the focus is not on the prompt, it's in the background or out of focus so the user just waits for something to happen. Sometimes this happens if users have dual screens, it pop's up on the second screen hidden or they don't realize it upntil they close the browser which closes the popup. You'll have to do more debugging, try using the Admin account wne viewing and see if the BOE Admin can browse the report.

entry.title: Re: What is the GS1-128 barcode format
entry.content: Hello @MahmoudElshall, I don't how you can manage it with serial number, but all what I know is that the GTIN number in the barcode should be on 14 characters. No less, no more. There is no separator ]C1 after the GTIN, so the system is not able to know where it stop if it's not on 14 characters. Regards Emmanuel

entry.title: Migration Template not available for App " Manage Rentable Object" in SAP CLM
entry.content:
In SAP Contract and Lease Management, Migration Template is not available for Application " Manage Rentable Object" but the Template is available for Application "Manage usable Object". How to get it, Please guide.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration Template not available for App " Manage Ren...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

There is an SAP Help Portal article available which lists out all available migration object in a searchable, filterable way. You can immediately click through the migration objects to get access to their specific documentations and prerequisites as well.

And in case you cannot find a migration object listed as available, you can follow the process outlined in KBA 2676589 to learn more about how you can submit feature requests related to Data Migration activities.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Ana_Figueira, 

I would recommend to use a Factory calendar, which is set up with daily dates and answering your requirements of execution, for the Delta accruals in the condition contract. When generating the calendar using factory calendar, the system will create every single Settlement date accordingly. 

You can then execute the Settlement via the corresponding tile, and set up a job with a variant created with dynamic date selection for the settlement date if required. 

You also have an option to use the Unplanned Settlement tile available after 2023 version I believe. This tile is pretty much the same as the first one but will create the Settlement date entry in the contract if it doesn't exist. 

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Performance of SAP Screen Personas and RFUI
entry.content:
We’re planning to leverage SAP Screen Personas to modernize our RFUI in a large-scale warehouse environment. Although we’ve successfully implemented Personas on smaller projects, we’re concerned about potential performance impacts at this scale. We’re aware of the significant enhancements delivered in 2023, which is encouraging.

Could you advise on how to estimate any performance degradation based on:

  • The number of Personas scripts and UI modifications

  • The peak number of concurrent RFUI users

Are there benchmarks, sizing guidelines, or tools that would help us predict and mitigate any issues before go-live?

I realize this is a somewhat unusual question, but any clarity or references you can share would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your insights.
